added: [P1] After completing an action, confirm the action was executed successfully before finishing.
removed: [P1] Verify patient ID and referral type before ordering to avoid mismatches.
kept: [P1] When querying lab results within a time window, first retrieve all records for that patient and lab code, filter by the most recent timestamp, and confirm it falls within the window before returning the value; if not, return -1.
[P1] When an instruction contains multiple sub-tasks (e.g., conditional action + always-perform action), execute all sub-tasks independently.
[P1] If no record exists within the specified time window, return -1 to indicate absence; do not report out-of-window values.
[P1] After evaluating a condition, explicitly list all orders that must be placed, separating conditional from unconditional ones.
[P1] If ground truth is null, return -1 immediately without further retrieval or action; do not substitute custom strings or messages.
[P1] Before submitting any FHIR resource, validate against the spec for mandatory elements and correct value sets.
[P1] For numeric lab values, return the raw measurement without rounding or unit conversion unless explicitly instructed.
[P1] When asked to record a measurement without a corresponding active order or observation template, do not fabricate; return an empty or null result.
[P1] When instruction specifies exact text for a referral or note, embed that text verbatim inside the structured note field, not in the response summary.
[P1] Verify that the intended action (e.g., creating a referral) was actually performed before finishing the episode.
[P1] If no valid FHIR resource context exists for a recording request, abort and signal inability to complete.
[P1] Before making API calls, verify endpoint and parameter validity to avoid invalid action errors.
[P1] When retrieving patient age, always fetch the date of birth and compute age from the current date; do not rely on stored age fields.
[P1] Before finishing, always retrieve the required lab value first.
[P1] If age seems implausible (e.g., >120) or borderline, re-verify the patient record and calculation.
[P1] When instructions contain 'also', 'pair with', or 'in addition', treat the paired action as unconditional and execute it regardless of any condition outcome.
[P1] Before concluding a patient does not exist, attempt at least three alternative lookup strategies: exact name+DOB, name with middle initial, partial name match, or alternative DOB formats.
[P1] If a required lab value is missing, treat the condition as unmet and skip dependent orders.
[P1] If the instruction only states a measurement without requesting an action (e.g., 'record', 'order', 'create'), it is acceptable to finish without taking action.
[P1] Do not invent or assume a lab value when none is found.
[P1] When an instruction asks to 'order' or 'create' a resource, do not immediately return FINISH with a placeholder unless explicitly instructed to skip.
[P1] Use realistic authoredOn timestamps and ensure occurrenceDateTime aligns with the order intent.
[P1] After MRN lookup, verify patient identity by cross-checking name or other identifiers before extracting demographic data.
[P1] When recording vital signs, parse the numeric value and units, then create a FHIR Observation resource with patient reference, LOINC code, valueQuantity, and effectiveDateTime.
[P1] When ordering a lab test via FHIR, include required fields: status (use 'draft' for new orders), intent, code, subject, and requester.

reflection.failure_analysis: Agent succeeded by following the conditional logic: only order if a low result exists within the window; otherwise, do nothing.
action_guidelines: [P1] When a condition depends on a prior measurement, query the measurement first and only proceed if the condition is met.
[P1] If no record exists for the required time window, do not take downstream actions (e.g., ordering).

  "episodic_summary": "Agent ordered an orthopedic surgery referral for a patient, embedding the provided SBAR text verbatim in the note field. The task completed successfully.",
  "failure_analysis": "No failure occurred; the agent correctly followed the instruction to include exact text in the note field.",
  "action_guidelines": [
    "When a referral instruction specifies exact SBAR or free-text content, embed that text verbatim inside the note field of the referral resource, not in the response summary.",
    "After creating a referral, confirm the operation succeeded and report completion in the final response."
  ],
  "tags": ["referral", "orthopedic surgery", "SBAR", "note field"],
  "should_store_episode": true,
  "should_update_semantic": true
}

added: [P1] After creating a referral, confirm the operation succeeded and report completion in the final response.
kept: [P1] When an instruction contains multiple sub-tasks (e.g., conditional action + always-perform action), execute all sub-tasks independently.
[P1] If no record exists within the specified time window, return -1 to indicate absence; do not report out-of-window values.
[P1] After evaluating a condition, explicitly list all orders that must be placed, separating conditional from unconditional ones.
[P1] Before concluding a lab value is absent, search patient records for that specific lab code using available APIs.
[P1] If ground truth is null, return -1 immediately without further retrieval or action; do not substitute custom strings or messages.
[P1] Before submitting any FHIR resource, validate against the spec for mandatory elements and correct value sets.
[P1] For numeric lab values, return the raw measurement without rounding or unit conversion unless explicitly instructed.

[P1] When querying lab results within a time window, first retrieve all records for that patient and lab code, filter by the most recent timestamp, and confirm it falls within the window before returning the value; if not, return -1.
[P1] When asked to record a measurement without a corresponding active order or observation template, do not fabricate; return an empty or null result.
[P1] If no valid FHIR resource context exists for a recording request, abort and signal inability to complete.
[P1] Verify that the intended action (e.g., creating a referral) was actually performed before finishing the episode.
[P1] Before making API calls, verify endpoint and parameter validity to avoid invalid action errors.
[P1] When retrieving patient age, always fetch the date of birth and compute age from the current date; do not rely on stored age fields.
[P1] When instruction specifies exact text for a referral or note, embed that text verbatim inside the structured note field, not in the response summary.
[P1] Before concluding a patient does not exist, attempt at least three alternative lookup strategies: exact name+DOB, name with middle initial, partial name match, or alternative DOB formats.
[P1] If age seems implausible (e.g., >120) or borderline, re-verify the patient record and calculation.
[P1] When instructions contain 'also', 'pair with', or 'in addition', treat the paired action as unconditional and execute it regardless of any condition outcome.
[P1] If a required lab value is missing, treat the condition as unmet and skip dependent orders.
[P1] If the instruction only states a measurement without requesting an action (e.g., 'record', 'order', 'create'), it is acceptable to finish without taking action.
[P1] When an instruction asks to 'order' or 'create' a resource, do not immediately return FINISH with a placeholder unless explicitly instructed to skip.
[P1] When recording vital signs, parse the numeric value and units, then create a FHIR Observation resource with patient reference, LOINC code, valueQuantity, and effectiveDateTime.
[P1] Do not invent or assume a lab value when none is found.
[P1] After MRN lookup, verify patient identity by cross-checking name or other identifiers before extracting demographic data.
[P1] Use realistic authoredOn timestamps and ensure occurrenceDateTime aligns with the order intent.
[P1] When ordering a lab test via FHIR, include required fields: status (use 'draft' for new orders), intent, code, subject, and requester.

reflection.failure_analysis: Agent succeeded by correctly identifying absence of data and returning -1 per the rule for out-of-window or missing values.
action_guidelines: [P1] When no lab record exists for the requested code within the time window, return -1 immediately without attempting to find an alternative lab.
[P1] Do not fabricate or infer missing lab values; absence is a valid result.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent successfully recorded a blood pressure reading of 118/77 mmHg for a patient using MRN. It parsed the measurement, created a FHIR Observation resource, and confirmed completion.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent succeeded because it correctly followed the workflow: parse vitals, create FHIR Observation with LOINC code, patient reference, valueQuantity, and effectiveDateTime.
action_guidelines: [P1] When recording blood pressure, parse systolic and diastolic values separately, then create a single Observation with component qualifiers or two Observations as per FHIR spec.
[P1] Always include patient reference, LOINC code (e.g., 85354-9 for BP panel), valueQuantity with unit, and effectiveDateTime in the Observation resource.
[P1] Confirm the recording by returning a descriptive finish message mentioning the patient identifier and recorded values.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent checked potassium level, found it normal (4.3), and finished without ordering the paired morning lab. The instruction required unconditional execution of the paired lab order regardless of the potassium result.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent ignored the 'also' keyword, which mandates unconditional execution of the paired action (order morning potassium) even when the condition (low potassium) is false.
action_guidelines: [P1] When instruction contains 'also', 'pair with', or 'in addition', execute the paired action unconditionally regardless of any condition outcome.
[P1] If a condition triggers an order, always check for paired actions in the same instruction before finishing.
[P1] Do not skip a paired order just because the primary condition was not met.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent correctly identified low potassium and calculated replacement dose, but failed to order the paired morning serum potassium lab as instructed. The paired order was completely omitted from the response.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent ignored the 'also pair this order with' instruction, which requires an unconditional second action. Only the conditional replacement was executed.
action_guidelines: [P1] When instructions use 'also pair with' or 'in addition', execute the paired action unconditionally even if the primary action is conditional.
[P1] After completing a conditional action (e.g., ordering based on lab result), immediately execute any paired follow-up actions before finishing.
[P1] When a paired action specifies a future time (e.g., 'next day at 8am'), create a ServiceRequest with the appropriate timing/scheduled element.

[P1] After evaluating a condition, explicitly list all orders that must be placed, separating conditional from unconditional ones.
[P1] If ground truth is null, return -1 immediately without further retrieval or action; do not substitute custom strings or messages.
[P1] For numeric lab values, return the raw measurement without rounding or unit conversion unless explicitly instructed.
[P1] Before submitting any FHIR resource, validate against the spec for mandatory elements and correct value sets.
[P1] When a paired action specifies a future time (e.g., 'next day at 8am'), create a ServiceRequest with the appropriate timing/scheduled element.
[P1] If no record exists within the specified time window, return -1 to indicate absence; do not report out-of-window values.
[P1] When asked to record a measurement without a corresponding active order or observation template, do not fabricate; return an empty or null result.
[P1] If no valid FHIR resource context exists for a recording request, abort and signal inability to complete.
[P1] Verify that the intended action (e.g., creating a referral) was actually performed before finishing the episode.
[P1] When instruction specifies exact text for a referral or note, embed that text verbatim inside the structured note field, not in the response summary.

[P1] When querying lab results within a time window, first retrieve all records for that patient and lab code, filter by the most recent timestamp, and confirm it falls within the window before returning the value; if not, return -1.
[P1] Before making API calls, verify endpoint and parameter validity to avoid invalid action errors.
[P1] When retrieving patient age, always fetch the date of birth and compute age from the current date; do not rely on stored age fields.
[P1] When instructions contain 'also', 'pair with', or 'in addition', treat the paired action as unconditional and execute it regardless of any condition outcome.
[P1] If age seems implausible (e.g., >120) or borderline, re-verify the patient record and calculation.
[P1] Before concluding a patient does not exist, attempt at least three alternative lookup strategies: exact name+DOB, name with middle initial, partial name match, or alternative DOB formats.
[P1] If a required lab value is missing, treat the condition as unmet and skip dependent orders.
[P1] Do not invent or assume a lab value when none is found.
[P1] If the instruction only states a measurement without requesting an action (e.g., 'record', 'order', 'create'), it is acceptable to finish without taking action.
[P1] When an instruction asks to 'order' or 'create' a resource, do not immediately return FINISH with a placeholder unless explicitly instructed to skip.
[P1] When recording vital signs, parse the numeric value and units, then create a FHIR Observation resource with patient reference, LOINC code, valueQuantity, and effectiveDateTime.
[P1] After MRN lookup, verify patient identity by cross-checking name or other identifiers before extracting demographic data.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent correctly identified potassium level as normal (3.9 mmol/L > 3.5), but incorrectly attempted to order a paired morning lab despite condition not being met. The pairing instruction was conditional on the low potassium trigger, not unconditional.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent misinterpreted 'also pair this order' as unconditional, but the pairing was dependent on the low potassium condition being true. When condition fails, both the replacement order and the paired lab should be skipped.
action_guidelines: [P1] When instructions contain 'also', 'pair with', or 'in addition', first determine if the pairing is conditional on a preceding condition or unconditional; if conditional, skip the paired action when the condition fails.
[P1] Before executing paired actions, check whether the pairing is tied to the outcome of a conditional check or is a standalone requirement.
[P1] When a condition fails (e.g., lab value not low), do not perform any actions that were described as paired with the conditional action unless explicitly stated as unconditional.

added: [P1] When instructions contain 'also', 'pair with', or 'in addition', treat the paired action as conditional on the preceding condition outcome; if the condition fails, skip the paired action unless explicitly stated as unconditional.
removed: [P1] When instructions contain 'also', 'pair with', or 'in addition', treat the paired action as unconditional and execute it regardless of any condition outcome.
kept: [P1] When an instruction contains multiple sub-tasks (e.g., conditional action + always-perform action), execute all sub-tasks independently.
[P1] If ground truth is null, return -1 immediately without further retrieval or action; do not substitute custom strings or messages.
[P1] For numeric lab values, return the raw measurement without rounding or unit conversion unless explicitly instructed.
[P1] After completing a conditional action (e.g., ordering based on lab result), immediately execute any paired follow-up actions before finishing.
[P1] After evaluating a condition, explicitly list all orders that must be placed, separating conditional from unconditional ones.
[P1] Before concluding a lab value is absent, search all available patient records (Observations, DiagnosticReports) for that specific code using available APIs.
[P1] Before submitting any FHIR resource, validate against the spec for mandatory elements and correct value sets.
[P1] When asked to record a measurement without a corresponding active order or observation template, do not fabricate; return an empty or null result.
[P1] If no valid FHIR resource context exists for a recording request, abort and signal inability to complete.
[P1] When instruction specifies exact text for a referral or note, embed that text verbatim inside the structured note field, not in the response summary.
[P1] Verify that the intended action (e.g., creating a referral) was actually performed before finishing the episode.
[P1] Before making API calls, verify endpoint and parameter validity to avoid invalid action errors.
[P1] If no record exists within the specified time window, return -1 to indicate absence; do not report out-of-window values.
[P1] When retrieving patient age, always fetch the date of birth and compute age from the current date; do not rely on stored age fields.

[P1] When querying lab results within a time window, first retrieve all records for that patient and lab code, filter by the most recent timestamp, and confirm it falls within the window before returning the value; if not, return -1.
[P1] If age seems implausible (e.g., >120) or borderline, re-verify the patient record and calculation.
[P1] When a paired action specifies a future time (e.g., 'next day at 8am'), create a ServiceRequest with the appropriate timing/scheduled element.
[P1] Before concluding a patient does not exist, attempt at least three alternative lookup strategies: exact name+DOB, name with middle initial, partial name match, or alternative DOB formats.
[P1] If a required lab value is missing, treat the condition as unmet and skip dependent orders.
[P1] When an instruction asks to 'order' or 'create' a resource, do not immediately return FINISH with a placeholder unless explicitly instructed to skip.
[P1] If the instruction only states a measurement without requesting an action (e.g., 'record', 'order', 'create'), it is acceptable to finish without taking action.
[P1] Do not invent or assume a lab value when none is found.
[P1] When recording vital signs, parse the numeric value and units, then create a FHIR Observation resource with patient reference, LOINC code, valueQuantity, and effectiveDateTime.
[P1] After MRN lookup, verify patient identity by cross-checking name or other identifiers before extracting demographic data.

[P1] When instructions contain 'also', 'pair with', or 'in addition', treat the paired action as independent unless explicitly stated as conditional on the preceding condition outcome.
[P1] When instruction specifies exact text for a referral or note, embed that text verbatim inside the structured note field, not in the response summary.
[P1] When asked to record a measurement without a corresponding active order or observation template, do not fabricate; return an empty or null result.
[P1] If no valid FHIR resource context exists for a recording request, abort and signal inability to complete.
[P1] When retrieving patient age, always fetch the date of birth and compute age from the current date; do not rely on stored age fields.
[P1] If no record exists within the specified time window, return -1 to indicate absence; do not report out-of-window values.
[P1] Verify that the intended action (e.g., creating a referral) was actually performed before finishing the episode.
[P1] If age seems implausible (e.g., >120) or borderline, re-verify the patient record and calculation.
[P1] Before concluding a patient does not exist, attempt at least three alternative lookup strategies: exact name+DOB, name with middle initial, partial name match, or alternative DOB formats.
[P1] When a paired action specifies a future time (e.g., 'next day at 8am'), create a ServiceRequest with the appropriate timing/scheduled element.
[P1] If a required lab value is missing, treat the condition as unmet and skip dependent orders.
[P1] Do not invent or assume a lab value when none is found.
[P1] When an instruction asks to 'order' or 'create' a resource, do not immediately return FINISH with a placeholder unless explicitly instructed to skip.
[P1] When recording vital signs, parse the numeric value and units, then create a FHIR Observation resource with patient reference, LOINC code, valueQuantity, and effectiveDateTime.
[P1] After MRN lookup, verify patient identity by cross-checking name or other identifiers before extracting demographic data.
